query: Ursula von der Leyen October 2025 critical raw materials rare earth elements strategy target country

{"query":"Ursula von der Leyen October 2025 critical raw materials rare earth elements strategy target country","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2080,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2025/10/25/european-union-seeks-to-end-dependency-on-china-for-rare-earths","title":"European Union seeks to end dependency on China for rare earths | Euronews","content":"Through the RESourceEU plan, the European Union seeks to end its reliance on China for its critical raw materials, which are essential for Europe's automotive, defence, Greentech and digital sectors.
The European Union is working on a strategy to reduce its dependency on Chinese critical raw materials, European Commission Ursula von der Leyen said on Saturday.
The renewed strategy comes in a response to China's decision in October to implement tighter restrictions on rare earths exports.
Von der Leyen said that in the short term, the EU would focus on finding solutions with its Chinese counterparts, but that it would also ramp up its efforts to coordinate with other countries to secure access to alternative sources of the minerals.
The European Commission chief said the new scheme, named RESourceEU, takes after REPowerEU, an intiative launched after Russia's invasion of Ukraine with the aim of strengthening the EU's independence in the energy sector, away from Russian energy sources."},{"url":"https://www.chemistryworld.com/news/eu-plots-course-to-secure-raw-materials-vital-to-a-low-carbon-economy/4016263.article","title":"EU plots course to secure raw materials vital to a low-carbon economy | News | Chemistry World","content":"The European commission has proposed legislation to secure the supply of crucial minerals such as lithium and rare earth elements (REEs) to the EU.
She noted that almost 90% of rare earths and 60% of lithium are processed in China. ‘Lithium and rare earths are already replacing gas and oil at the heart of our economy,’ she said. ‘By 2030, our demand for those rare earth metals will increase fivefold.’
The Russian invasion of Ukraine and its subsequent political leveraging of Europe’s reliance on Russian hydrocarbons spooked many countries, and placed renewed focus on the reliability of supply for critical resources.
Russia used to account for 40% of gas imports to the EU, but it is now down to 9% of pipeline gas, von der Leyen noted. ‘Russia keeps on actively manipulating our energy market.
In her speech, von der Leyen namechecked new agreements with Chile, Mexico and New Zealand, and noted negotiations were at an advanced stage with Australia and India."},{"url":"https://www.politico.eu/article/ursula-von-der-leyen-new-plan-break-china-critical-materials/","title":"Von der Leyen touts new plan to break reliance on China critical minerals – POLITICO","content":"The European Commission will present a new plan to break the EU's dependencies on China for critical raw materials, President Ursula von der Leyen announced on Saturday.
But she said it would follow a similar model as the REPowerEU plan that the Commission introduced in 2022 to phase out Russian fossil fuels after Moscow's illegal invasion of Ukraine.
The aim of RESourceEU "is to secure access to alternative sources of critical raw materials in the short, medium and long term for our European industry," von der Leyen explained.
She added that the EU "will speed up work on critical raw materials partnerships with countries like Ukraine and Australia, Canada, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Chile and Greenland.""},{"url":"https://table.media/en/europe/news/resourceeu-von-der-leyen-announces-new-plan-for-critical-raw-materials","title":"RESourceEU: Von der Leyen announces new plan for critical raw materials • Table.Briefings","content":"The EU Commission wants to reduce Europe’s dependence on China.
At the Berlin Global Dialogue, the president presented “RESourceEU,” a new program designed to secure raw materials and promote recycling."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Ursula von der Leyen October 2025 critical raw materials rare earth elements strategy target country","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://europeannewsroom.com/eu-races-to-reduce-rare-earths-dependence-on-china-amid-trade-clash","title":"EU races to reduce rare earths dependence on China amid trade clash | European Newsroom","content":"While the EU is hoping to resolve a trade dispute with China over critical raw materials – like lithium, cobalt, nickel, and other rare earth elements – it is already looking to diversify its supply and boost its in-house extraction and production. The European Union is rushing to resolve a trade dispute with China concerning export controls on rare earths. A spokesman for the European Commission said on Thursday in Brussels: “In principle, we welcome any development that removes barriers to global trade flows.” He however added that “the EU is fully focused on its own bilateral trade engagement with China, including – as this relates to trade in – rare earth minerals” and confirmed that high-level technical discussions will take place in Brussels this Friday (October 31, 2025). The crisis has prompted European Commission President **Ursula von der Leyen** on Saturday to advocate for a package of measures to rapidly reduce Europe’s dependence on raw material imports from China.","score":0.7756902,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/china/eu-steps-up-efforts-cut-reliance-chinese-rare-earths-2025-10-25","title":"EU steps up efforts to cut reliance on Chinese rare earths","content":"Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv. ## Browse World. *   Ukraine and Russia at War. ## Browse Business. ## Browse Markets. BRUSSELS, Oct 25 (Reuters) - The European Union is developing a new plan to curb its reliance on Chinese critical raw materials, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said on Saturday, as she criticised Beijing for its expanded restrictions on the export of rare earths. \"The aim is to secure access to alternative sources of critical raw materials in the short, medium and long term for our European industries,\" she told a conference in Berlin. We will boost investment in strategic projects for the production and processing of critical raw materials here in the European Union,\" she added. *   #### World-Check, opens new tab. *   About Reuters, opens new tab. *   Media Center, opens new tab.","score":0.7487387,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/infographics/critical-raw-materials","title":"Critical raw materials act - consilium.europa.eu","content":"China provides 100% of the EU's supply of heavy rare earth elements · Turkey provides 99% of the EU's supply of boron · South Africa provides 71% of the EU's","score":0.6072213,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.giga-hamburg.de/en/publications/giga-focus/europe-s-quest-for-critical-raw-materials-in-latin-america","title":"Europe's Quest for Critical Raw Materials in Latin America","content":"In April 2025 the Chinese government expanded export controls to include seven heavy rare-earth elements, as well as related compounds, metals, and magnets.","score":0.47139415,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.politico.eu/article/eu-commission-wants-escape-china-grip-critical-rare-minerals-can-it-afford-to","title":"The EU wants to escape China's grip on critical minerals. Can it afford to?","content":"Ursula von der Leyen has been forced to act by Beijing's expansion and tightening of export controls on rare earths and other critical minerals","score":0.4549328,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.87,"request_id":"4d59d4b2-fd33-4ce8-8f03-34b17efcb673"}
